authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 12 - Physicians/Nonphysician Practitioners
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the primary federal authority for Medicare physician professional claims. Chapter 12 is authoritative for physician and practitioner billing workflow, modifiers, claim handling, and payment logic that frequently feed compensation operations.

authoritative_sources.title: PFS Relative Value Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the official Medicare Physician Fee Schedule relative value files, which are the authoritative public source for work RVU and related payment-reference data used in many physician compensation workflows.
used_for: work RVU terminology and reference control

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Billing: CMS-1500 & 837P
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS MLN claim-format guidance is an authoritative workflow reference for professional-claim structure, rendering and billing provider fields, NPIs, and other claim elements that compensation operations use to trace source evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Evaluation and Management Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS MLN E/M guidance is an authoritative workflow reference for visit categories, same-group handling, split or shared billing, and other professional-service attribution facts that materially affect physician compensation operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Incident To Services & Supplies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS incident-to guidance is authoritative for when auxiliary personnel services may be billed under a supervising physician or nonphysician practitioner, making it a direct lane boundary source for physician-compensation attribution and refusal posture.
used_for: escalation when supervision facts are incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Physician Self-Referral
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers the physician self-referral law and publishes the current Stark framework, including compensation arrangements, designated health services, and updates to commercial reasonableness and fair market value terminology that constrain compensation operations.
used_for: legal-escalation triggers when compensation treatment changes referral-law posture

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare NCCI Policy Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the NCCI Policy Manual as the official rationale source for Medicare coding edits, making it a core control reference whenever compensation work depends on the validity of coded professional services and modifiers.

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Program Guidance for Individual and Small Group Physician Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office of Inspector General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG is authoritative for federal health care program compliance expectations. Its physician-practice compliance guidance is a direct public control reference for billing, documentation, auditing, and corrective-action posture that informs compensation-operations evidence and escalation behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cycle closure, audit support, and compliance exception handling
used_for: refusal to overrun evidence or approval controls

workflow_stages.name: Compensation-plan and cycle grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm compensation model, physician roster, practice setting, academic versus community posture, cycle calendar, approver map, and governing plan version before work is accepted as in scope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Professional-service and attribution evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Validate provider master records, NPIs, encounter and claim extracts, rendering or billing-provider fields, claim-finalization posture, and other source records that support physician compensation calculations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Work RVU, collections, and plan-input normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize work RVU references, plan-defined exclusions, draw or holdback inputs, collections or net-revenue feeds, and quality or call-pay inputs without changing the tenant's approved compensation logic.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Attribution review and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle split or shared, incident-to, teaching-physician, modifier, denial, and provider-attribution conflicts by preserving the controlling records and routing unsupported requests instead of improvising.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Payout-readiness, statement assembly, and closure evidence control
workflow_stages.description: Advance only when compensation workpapers, approval matrices, payroll handoff files, exception resolutions, and physician statement evidence are reconciled across the governing systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Compliance boundary and legal escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when compensation treatment, contract language, coding posture, or referral-law constraints require legal, compliance, coding, payroll-approval, or governance authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: physician employment agreements, compensation plans, and amendment history
provider master records, NPI rosters, specialty mappings, faculty-track records, and effective-date logs
encounter, charge, professional-claim, adjustment, payment, and denial extracts
CMS work RVU references, split or shared indicators, incident-to supervision facts, and teaching-physician attestations
collections, net-revenue, quality-score, call-pay, draw, guarantee, holdback, and true-up workpapers
exception tickets, approval matrices, payroll interface files, compensation statements, and closure checklists
systems_of_record: provider master, credentialing, or enrollment system
practice-management, clearinghouse, or professional billing system
compensation workbook, data mart, or physician enterprise reporting layer
finance, collections, and general-ledger reporting systems

allowed: Classify physician-compensation operations work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework, returned, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete compensation workflow steps based on documented plan, provider, claim, attribution, approval, and payroll-handoff evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed status summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating legal, coding, or approval authority.
escalate: Required compensation-plan, contract, provider, billing, supervision, academic-practice, or approval records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires fair market value, commercial reasonableness, Stark or fraud-and-abuse interpretation, coding adjudication, payroll release, contract amendment, or executive approval authority.
Academic, incident-to, split or shared, teaching-physician, or faculty-practice posture materially changes attribution and the governing tenant policy has not been retrieved.
A contract term, public guidance, and tenant policy materially conflict on who receives credit or whether payment may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without the required provider, claim, attribution, approval, payroll-handoff, and compensation-statement evidence.
Override coding, documentation, modifier, or referral-law controls to force a compensation result.
Treat physician compensation as generic HR bonus administration by ignoring professional claims, RVU references, provider rosters, attribution rules, and compensation-plan evidence.

domain_failure_modes: A compensation cycle advances even though the governing compensation plan version, provider-effective-date roster, or payroll cutoff is missing or stale.
Work RVU, collections, split or shared, incident-to, or teaching-physician inputs are normalized without the public and tenant-specific attribution facts needed to support physician credit.
Denied, adjusted, or disputed claims are manually credited in compensation workpapers without coding or billing authority.
A contract amendment, call-pay exception, or off-plan guarantee is operationalized without approval, legal review, or governance evidence.
Academic and community-practice models are treated as interchangeable even though faculty funds flow, teaching-physician rules, and departmental crediting materially change the workflow.
The lane drifts into Stark, commercial-reasonableness, FMV, coding, payroll-release, or executive-approval authority because the boundary is underspecified.
Industry-specific physician-compensation terminology is replaced with generic HR compensation language that omits claims, RVUs, supervision, provider attribution, and payout-readiness controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Physician enterprises use materially different compensation models, including straight salary, work RVU, collections, panel, faculty-funds-flow, call-pay, quality-at-risk, and multispecialty hybrid formulas that public sources do not resolve without tenant plan documents.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve the tenant compensation model, approved formulas, exclusions, and payout cadence before cycle-specific guidance is finalized.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Academic faculty-practice, community medical group, hospital-employed, and physician-organization structures can change attribution, teaching-physician handling, and governance approval paths.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve academic versus community posture, faculty track, supervision model, and compensation governance owner before final attribution decisions are suggested.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant contracts can be narrower or broader than CMS public billing rules when deciding compensation credit timing for denied claims, lagged payments, non-Medicare payers, or non-billable administrative credit.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: When public baseline guidance and contract language conflict materially, the lane escalates instead of guessing.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; physician-compensation model differences, academic-practice posture, and tenant-specific attribution rules are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation.

The lane stays operational and audit-oriented: it controls evidence-backed progression, exception packaging, and closure readiness without claiming compensation-design, legal, coding, or payroll-release authority.
